About: It is about the John Frost Bridge.
Affects: It affects the Lower Rhine at Arnhem.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Major-General John Dutton Frost.
HasA: It has a temporary replacement pontoon bridge.
It has a higher Bailey bridge.
It has a new Rijnbrug rebuilt in exactly the same style as the destroyed bridge.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being named after Major-General John Dutton Frost.
It is characterized by its temporary defense during the Battle of Arnhem.
It is characterized by being bombed and destroyed by Martin B-26 Marauders.
It is characterized by being rebuilt in exactly the same style as the destroyed bridge.
It is characterized by its renaming on 17 December 1977.
It is characterized by hosting the World Liberty Concert on 8 May 1995.
It is characterized by being one of the largest memorial concerts ever held in the Netherlands.
HeldIn: It was held in the Netherlands.
IsA: It is a permanent link across the Lower Rhine.
KnownAs: It is known as John Frostbrug.
KnownFor: It is known for the Battle of Arnhem.
It is known for being referenced in the film A Bridge Too Far.
LocatedIn: It is located over the Lower Rhine.
NamedAfter: It is named after Major-General John Dutton Frost.
OccurredAt: It occurred at the northern end of the bridge.
It occurred at the southern end of the bridge.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the invasion of the Netherlands.
It occurred during the Battle of Arnhem.
It occurred during Operation Market Garden.
It occurred during the German counter offensive against the Allied bridgehead.
PartOf: It is part of the Netherlands.
PerformedBy: It was performed by Dutch engineers.
It was performed by the British 1st Airborne Division.
It was performed by Lt-Colonel John Frost.
It was performed by German forces.
It was performed by Martin B-26 Marauders of the 344th Bomb Group.
It was performed by the World Liberty Concert.
UsedAs: It is used as a temporary replacement.
It is used as a temporary link.
It is used as a temporary replacement for a floating bridge.
It is used as a temporary replacement for a road bridge.
It is used as a temporary replacement for a higher Bailey bridge.
It is used as a temporary replacement for a new Rijnbrug.
UsedFor: It is used for slowing the German advance.
It is used for sending reinforcements south of the river.
It is used for hosting memorial concerts.
UsedIn: It is used in the Netherlands.
UsedTo: It is used to slow the German advance.
It is used to send reinforcements south of the river.
It is used to pass underneath.
